spark.name: Spark
spark.description: A snapping bolt of static light, conjured from the air and flung at a foe. The first cantrip every delver learns — cheap, quick, and bright enough to sting. It strikes true so long as you can see your mark; only a creature's Ward blunts it.

regeneration.name: Regeneration
regeneration.description: A slow weave of mending light, drawn up by the caster's own will and sunk beneath the skin, where it knits torn flesh a little at a time. It cannot be hurled at a foe — only laid on the willing, be it your own hand or an ally's. Cast it on yourself, a fellow delver, or any creature you can see; the deeper your Intellect, the more it knits with each pulse.

glimmerskin.name: Glimmerskin
glimmerskin.description: A wardweave that draws a pinch of raw glimmer up out of the shards and crusts it over the skin in a thin, faintly luminous shell — part plate, part charm. It dulls a blade's bite and blunts hostile magic alike, but the glimmer burns away as it works, so the weave costs shards to cast. Lay it on yourself, an ally, or any creature you can see; the keener your Intellect, the thicker the crust. Re-casting renews the shell rather than doubling it.

summon-wisp.name: Summon Wisp
summon-wisp.description: A weave that conjures a knot of cold light into a biddable shape and binds it to your will for a time. The conjured wisp drifts at your heel and spits its light at your foes, but the binding frays — in a few minutes it slips loose and the wisp unravels. Cast it and a Wisp answers; cast it again and the old one lets go as the new takes shape.

candlelight.name: Candlelight
candlelight.description: The humblest of human light-cantrips, taught to hedge-mages and night-travellers the world over: a delver kindles a mote of cold flame above the brow, just bright enough to push the dark back a single step. It sheds the barest light and gutters out within the minute, but in a black drift it is the difference between blind and not. Cast it on yourself, an ally, or any creature you can see.

mage-armour.name: Mage Armour
mage-armour.description: A wardweave of pure will rather than glimmer — the caster draws a lattice of hardened light close about a body, an invisible barding that turns a blade a fraction before it bites. It costs no shards, only concentration, and holds for a few minutes before it thins to nothing. The keener your Intellect, the tighter the weave. Lay it on yourself, an ally, or any creature you can see; re-casting renews the lattice rather than stacking it.

sleep.name: Sleep
sleep.description: A hush of drowsy light poured into a foe's eyes, weighting its limbs until it folds where it stands and sinks into slumber. It deals no harm — its mercy is the opening it buys: a chance to slip away, or to set a killing blow before the sleeper stirs. Any blow rouses it at once, and a creature's Ward may shrug the weave off entirely. Cast it at a creature you can see.

bolt.name: Bolt
bolt.description: Where Spark merely stings, Bolt is a hurled lance of compressed light — slower to shape and hungrier for mana, but it lands with real weight and punches deep. The first true war-spell a delver learns, scaling hard with Intellect; only a creature's Ward turns it aside. Cast it at any foe you can see.

glimmer-spike.name: Glimmer Spike
glimmer-spike.description: The mage's heaviest single strike: a spike of raw glimmer dragged up out of the shards themselves and driven through a foe like a spear of frozen light. It burns mana and shards together, for the glimmer is spent in the casting and cannot be reclaimed — but nothing else a delver can throw bites as deep. It scales with Intellect, and Ward alone can blunt it. Cast it at any foe you can see.
